differentials.name: Early branching of pulmonary vein
differentials.raw: Early branching of pulmonary vein (< 1 cm)

context: √  aberrant insertion with perpendicular orientation to posterior left atrial wall Mean size of ostium:   9.9 ± 1.9 mm Strong association with:   atrial fibrillation Clinical significance: LA muscle (as a focus of ectopic electrical activity) can extend into venous ostia → atrial fibrillation → treatment with radiofrequency catheter ablation Pulmonary veins contribute to the origination and maintenance of atrial fibrillation, most commonly with a focus in the left superior pulmonary vein! ◊  Alert cardiologist about variant anatomy of additional pulmonary veins prior to RF ablation!
